channel.title: Eric Lambert, build notes
channel.description: Short posts about what I am learning in my CS program, the budget tracker I am building, and the occasional help desk war story.

item.title: Why my budget app kept lying about percentages
item.description: A bug where my monthly summary divided by the wrong total whenever there were two income sources in the same month. The fix was small. Finding it took a whole evening and a lot of print statements.

item.title: Linked lists finally clicked for the students I tutor
item.description: The treasure hunt analogy beats every diagram I have tried. Each node only knows where the next clue is. You cannot skip ahead. Once they draw it themselves, the pointer logic stops being scary.

item.title: Reading my first binary bomb
item.description: Intro to Systems is the most uncomfortable and most interesting class I have taken. Staring at assembly with a debugger open is humbling, but watching a high level line map down to registers makes the whole machine feel real.

item.title: Moving the budget tracker from a script to a small web app
item.description: The command line version did its job, but a couple of friends wanted to try it. That means a real interface, persistent storage that does not corrupt, and graceful errors when the data file is missing. Notes on what I am learning as I go.

item.title: What a semester at the help desk taught me about patience
item.description: Most tickets are not really about the printer. They are about a person who is already late and now also stuck. Slowing down and explaining the why, not just the fix, changed how my shifts go.

item.title: Big-O without the panic
item.description: Most students get the intuition faster than they think. Constant time means the steps do not grow. Linear grows with the data. The formal definition can wait until the picture is solid.
